title: Variability of growth parameter estimates - The role of rescaling and reparametrization
one_sentence_summary: Baranyi & Rockaya show that for the Baranyi-Roberts primary growth model the error structure after reparametrization is approximately linear so t-distribution-based standard errors give realistic confidence intervals — closest existing prior art on growth-curve CI calibration, but considers only the well-specified-model case.

title: A tutorial on uncertainty propagation techniques for predictive microbiology models
one_sentence_summary: Tutorial benchmarking sigma-point, Monte Carlo, linearisation, and bootstrap propagation of parameter uncertainty through Baranyi-style growth models — focuses on propagation accuracy rather than frequentist coverage of CIs under structural misspecification.

title: Comparing design of experiments and optimal experimental design techniques for modelling microbial growth
one_sentence_summary: Compares experimental designs for estimating growth-model parameters and quantifies the resulting parameter uncertainties.

title: The importance of what we cannot observe: Experimental limitations as a source of bias for meta-regression models in predictive microbiology
one_sentence_summary: Demonstrates that experimental detection limits and censoring induce systematic bias in fitted growth-model parameters — a misspecification of the data-generating process rather than the deterministic model, but closely related to our concerns.

title: Shelf life estimation of refrigerated vacuum packed beef accounting for uncertainty
one_sentence_summary: Propagates parameter uncertainty from Gompertz-style spoilage fits to shelf-life predictions but does not validate the coverage of the resulting intervals.

title: The Most Probable Curve method - A robust approach to estimate kinetic models from low plate count data
one_sentence_summary: Robust estimation method for primary kinetic models when data are noisy/censored; discusses uncertainty but not coverage.

title: Multilevel modelling as a tool to include variability and uncertainty in quantitative microbiology
one_sentence_summary: Hierarchical/multilevel growth-curve models for separating biological from measurement variability; presents parameter intervals but does not measure their frequentist coverage.

title: Modelling Salmonella concentration throughout the pork supply chain considering growth and survival in fluctuating conditions
one_sentence_summary: Classic ComBase-era growth/survival model under fluctuating conditions; provides prediction uncertainty but no coverage validation.

title: Analysis and validation of a predictive model for growth and death of Aeromonas hydrophila
one_sentence_summary: Early ComBase-style validation study comparing predictions to independent data; uses RMSE-type metrics rather than CI coverage.

title: Estimation of uncertainty and variability in bacterial growth using Bayesian inference
one_sentence_summary: One of the earliest applications of Bayesian MCMC to bacterial growth-curve fitting; reports credible intervals but does not check frequentist coverage or sensitivity to prior/misspecification.

title: Stochastically modeling Listeria monocytogenes growth in farm tank milk
one_sentence_summary: Bayesian hierarchical model of Listeria growth with variability components; gives credible intervals on growth rates without coverage diagnostics.

title: Bayesian synthesis of a pathogen growth model: Listeria monocytogenes under competition
one_sentence_summary: Bayesian fit of Jameson-effect competition model to growth data with posterior parameter intervals — relevant Bayesian-growth-curve antecedent but no coverage analysis.

title: Bayesian modeling of two- and three-species bacterial competition in milk
one_sentence_summary: Bayesian competition growth model with prior sensitivity discussion; no coverage assessment.

title: Hierarchical Bayesian analysis of censored microbiological contamination data for use in risk assessment
one_sentence_summary: Hierarchical Bayesian modelling of censored contamination counts; emphasises uncertainty quantification rather than calibration.

title: Incorporating prior knowledge improves detection of differences in bacterial growth rate
one_sentence_summary: Bayesian growth-rate estimation showing informative priors improve inference on differences — a sensitivity-to-prior result relevant to our discussion.

title: A Bayesian approach to analyzing phenotype microarray data enables estimation of microbial growth parameters
one_sentence_summary: Bayesian primary-model fits to phenotype microarray growth curves; quantifies posterior parameter uncertainty.

title: Optimal experimental design for improving the estimation of growth parameters of Lactobacillus viridescens under non-isothermal conditions
one_sentence_summary: Uses OED on non-isothermal Baranyi fits to reduce parameter standard errors; assumes correct model.

title: Comparing design of experiments and optimal experimental design techniques for modelling microbial growth
one_sentence_summary: Compares D-/E-optimal and standard DoE for growth-model estimation; reports parameter precision (CV, SE) without coverage.

title: Modelling of microbial activity and prediction of shelf life for packed fresh fish
one_sentence_summary: Foundational shelf-life model with empirical validation against independent challenge tests; classic ComBase-era validation paradigm.

title: Structural and practical identifiability analysis of partially observed dynamical models by exploiting the profile likelihood
one_sentence_summary: Standard reference establishing the profile-likelihood approach to practical identifiability and CI construction in nonlinear ODE models — directly applicable to growth-curve fits.

title: Fast integration-based prediction bands for ordinary differential equation models
one_sentence_summary: Constructs prediction bands for ODE trajectories via profile-likelihood integration; directly applicable to growth-curve predictive intervals.

title: Monte Carlo profile confidence intervals for dynamic systems
journal: Journal of the Royal Society Interface
one_sentence_summary: Develops Monte Carlo profile CI methodology for noisy partially-observed dynamic systems with proof of nominal coverage under regularity.

title: Likelihood-ratio test statistic for the finite-sample case in nonlinear ordinary differential equation models
one_sentence_summary: Studies finite-sample LRT calibration for nonlinear ODE models and shows when chi-square approximation fails — directly bears on our claim that asymptotic CIs miscover at small N.

title: Practical parameter identifiability for spatio-temporal models of cell invasion
journal: Journal of the Royal Society Interface
one_sentence_summary: Demonstrates profile-likelihood identifiability and CI coverage for biological growth/invasion PDE/ODE models with simulation studies — close in spirit to our work but for cell-invasion models.

title: A confidence building exercise in data and identifiability: Modeling cancer chemotherapy as a case study
one_sentence_summary: Simulation study of identifiability and confidence-region coverage for biological-ODE fits; shows how unidentifiability inflates intervals.

title: An algorithm for computing profile likelihood based pointwise confidence intervals for nonlinear dose-response models
one_sentence_summary: Algorithm for profile-likelihood CIs in nonlinear dose-response (structurally similar to growth) with simulation-based coverage check.

title: Applications of likelihood asymptotics for nonlinear regression in herbicide bioassays
one_sentence_summary: Classic reference for higher-order likelihood (Skovgaard/modified profile) CIs in nonlinear regression including dose-response curves — methodological backbone for any small-sample nonlinear CI work.

title: Nonlinear Regression
one_sentence_summary: The standard textbook reference for inference in nonlinear regression including linearisation Wald intervals, likelihood-based intervals, and curvature diagnostics.

title: Nonlinear Regression Analysis and Its Applications
one_sentence_summary: Standard textbook covering intrinsic/parameter-effects curvature, profile traces, and the bias of Wald CIs in nonlinear regression — direct intellectual ancestor of any growth-curve CI critique.

title: Applied Asymptotics: Case Studies in Small-Sample Statistics
one_sentence_summary: Definitive case-study treatment of higher-order asymptotic CIs in nonlinear and non-Gaussian models including nlreg software — relevant to small-sample growth-curve fits.

title: Practical Bayesian model evaluation using leave-one-out cross-validation and WAIC
one_sentence_summary: Foundational PSIS-LOO paper introducing fast and stable Bayesian model evaluation with importance-weight diagnostics; essential citation for any modern Bayesian inference workflow.

title: Pareto Smoothed Importance Sampling
one_sentence_summary: Technical foundation of PSIS — generalized-Pareto tail fitting to stabilize importance weights with k-hat diagnostic for unreliable weights.

title: Validating Bayesian Inference Algorithms with Simulation-Based Calibration
one_sentence_summary: Defines SBC: rank statistics under data simulated from the prior should be uniform if the posterior sampler is correctly calibrated — the canonical way to detect mis-calibration of Bayesian intervals.

title: Bayesian sandwich posteriors for pseudo-true parameters
one_sentence_summary: Constructs Bayesian posteriors that target the pseudo-true parameter under model misspecification with asymptotic frequentist coverage — directly relevant theoretical backdrop for our misspec results.

title: Models as Approximations II: A Model-Free Theory of Parametric Regression
one_sentence_summary: Develops a model-free theory of parametric regression under misspecification including sandwich variance estimators — frequentist counterpart to the Hoff-Wakefield Bayesian sandwich.

title: Safe-Bayesian Generalized Linear Regression
one_sentence_summary: Establishes safe-Bayes (learning-rate-tempered) posteriors that concentrate around the KL-projection under misspecification — foundational generalised-Bayes reference.

title: Fast Rates for General Unbounded Loss Functions: from ERM to Generalized Bayes
one_sentence_summary: Theoretical convergence rates for generalised Bayes under misspecification across loss functions; underpins the safe-Bayes coverage story.

title: Calibrating generalized predictive distributions
one_sentence_summary: Generalised predictive calibration algorithm that tunes the learning rate so credible intervals achieve nominal coverage under misspecification.

title: A comparison of learning rate selection methods in generalized Bayesian inference
one_sentence_summary: Benchmarks learning-rate tuning methods for safe-Bayes using coverage probability as a primary metric — exactly the kind of calibration diagnostic we use.

title: Accurate Uncertainties for Deep Learning Using Calibrated Regression
one_sentence_summary: Post-hoc isotonic calibration of regression credible intervals to nominal coverage — a recalibration approach broadly relevant to our misspecification fix.

title: Posterior SBC: Simulation-Based Calibration Checking Conditional on Data
one_sentence_summary: Extends SBC to validate posterior inference conditional on the observed data — directly applicable to ODE-/growth-curve calibration checks.

title: Simulation-Based Calibration Checking for Bayesian Computation: The Choice of Test Quantities Shapes Sensitivity
one_sentence_summary: Refines SBC by using data-dependent test quantities; increases sensitivity to subtle miscalibration relevant to nonlinear/ODE fits.

title: Approximate leave-future-out cross-validation for Bayesian time series models
one_sentence_summary: PSIS-based approximate LFO-CV for time-series models — directly applicable to evaluating Bayesian growth-curve predictive performance.

title: Using stacking to average Bayesian predictive distributions
one_sentence_summary: Bayesian model stacking under M-open (i.e., misspecified) settings using PSIS weights; addresses misspecification by averaging rather than picking one model.

title: Generalized Bayes Approach to Inverse Problems with Model Misspecification
one_sentence_summary: Gibbs/safe-Bayes posterior framework for PDE-based inverse problems with model misspecification — methodologically analogous to misspecified ODE growth fits.

title: Bayesian sandwich posteriors for pseudo-true parameters
one_sentence_summary: Bayesian posteriors that are asymptotically calibrated to the pseudo-true parameter under misspecification — theoretical justification for sandwich-style adjustments to Bayesian CIs.

title: Utilization of mathematical models to manage risk of holding cold food without temperature control
one_sentence_summary: Risk-management application of predictive-microbiology models; demonstrates use but not statistical calibration.

title: Modeling of 2D diffusion processes based on microscopy data: parameter estimation and practical identifiability analysis
one_sentence_summary: Profile-likelihood-based parameter identifiability and CI construction for biological diffusion models — methodological cousin to growth-curve fits.

title: Optimal Experimental Design Based on Two-Dimensional Likelihood Profiles
one_sentence_summary: Uses 2D profile-likelihoods for OED in ODE models; closely related toolkit to ours.

title: Confidence intervals by constrained optimization—An algorithm and software package for practical identifiability analysis in systems biology
one_sentence_summary: Software-focused implementation of profile-likelihood CIs for systems-biology ODE models with explicit identifiability handling.

title: Parameter identifiability and model selection for sigmoid population growth models
one_sentence_summary: Profile-likelihood identifiability + model selection across sigmoid growth models — same model class as MisspecStudy, but does not measure CI coverage under cross-model misspecification.

title: Making Predictions Using Poorly Identified Mathematical Models
one_sentence_summary: Prediction with weakly identifiable nonlinear ODE models; close in spirit to misspec story, focuses on prediction-set construction.

title: Practical identifiability analysis of a minimal cardiovascular system model
one_sentence_summary: Identifiability + parameter-CI for ODE physiological model; methodology adjacent to growth ODE work.

title: Bootstrap prediction intervals for linear, nonlinear, and nonparametric autoregressions
one_sentence_summary: Foundational bootstrap-prediction-interval methodology; calibrated coverage in nonlinear regression with finite samples.

title: Fast bootstrap confidence intervals for continuous threshold linear regression
one_sentence_summary: Faster bootstrap CI for nonsmooth nonlinear regression; methodologically relevant for our bootstrap baseline.
